repo_purpose: This repository showcases various design patterns and their implementation in Java for educational purposes, along with documentation and testing modules.
architecture_summary: The repository is structured into multiple modules, each demonstrating a distinct design pattern. Notable modules include localization for Chinese documentation, hexagonal architecture for decoupled business logic, page object for automated UI testing, microservices API gateway for managing microservice interactions, and miscellaneous design patterns. Each module is developed with a specific focus, such as maintainability, testability, UI automation, or microservices communication, leveraging Java and various Maven dependencies for configuration. Challenges include incomplete test coverage and the need for clear documentation and implementation examples in some modules.

modules.name: localization
modules.responsibility: Provides localized versions of design patterns documentation in Chinese.

modules.name: hexagonal-architecture
modules.responsibility: Demonstrates the Hexagonal Architecture pattern, focusing on decoupling business logic from external interfaces.

modules.name: page-object
modules.responsibility: Implements the Page Object pattern in Java for automated UI testing.

modules.name: microservices-api-gateway
modules.responsibility: Implements the Microservices API Gateway pattern for optimizing communication between clients and microservices.

modules.name: misc
modules.responsibility: Hosts a variety of design pattern implementations and documentation, including specifications for use in Java.

entry_points.kind: Main entry point for routing requests

risks.description: No tests are present for LotteryAdministration and LotteryService classes in hexagonal-architecture module.
MongoEventLogTest is disabled, making tests non-executable in hexagonal-architecture.
Tests using WebClient may not have complete browser simulation in page-object module.
Missing tests for ApiGateway and its methods in microservices-api-gateway.
Some modules' documentation may lack clear examples and comprehensive test coverage.
